description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.
Maelstrom armor, inbuilt subdermally alongside exterior AJ.
A cybernetical replacement arm. Comes with a free hand.
Armored knuckles which function as a Melee Weapon.
A monowire with a serrated hook is fired from a wrist-mounted medium pistol, its feed reeling in automatically after a miss.
Armored knuckles which function as a Melee Weapon.
A cybernetical replacement arm. Comes with a free hand.
A concealable one-handed Ranged Weapon. Can't hold anything with this hand while activated.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 4x/Elite Specialist - Not exactly the rank-and-file, but not exactly a leadership figure, either.

Gang-Wide Ability - Aftermarket Sandevistan: At the start of each round of initiative, choose a Maelstrom gang member currently in combat. That gang member goes first, this round. Additionally, when rolling endurance checks for Maelstrom, use the physical skill instead of lowdown.

Ability - Linked Neuro-Dampeners: If a Maelstrom gang member takes damage in proximity to the ripper, the ripper is immediately informed of it. On their turn, the ripper may choose a member of their squad (including themselves) that has taken damage. That member regains 1 SP on a piece of armor (reflecting pain tolerance) and, if seriously wounded, they no longer suffer the roll penalty for the status condition.

Extra Gear - Choose one per brawler:

  • Bloodthirster (pop-out medium pistol with 1 shot and a 10m range): on a successful ranged attack the target is grappled. At the start of the brawler’s turn, they can choose to pull the target 5m towards the brawler.

    • A monowire with a serrated hook is fired from a wrist-mounted medium pistol, its feed reeling in instantly on a miss.
  • Devastator (improved melee weapons): add 1d6 to the damage inflicted by melee weapons. Any damage inflicted to objects, vehicles or robotics (not including cyberware) ignores armor.
  • Popout Very Heavy Pistol: one of the user’s arms is fitted with a popout Very Heavy Pistol.

Maelstrom’s hellish psychoes are dangerous enough on their own, but their rippers are another beast entirely. Heart and soul of the gang, they tune the chooms they work on to sync with their neuralware, literally let everyone feel each other’s pain like some kind fo fucked up chrome hivemind. Makes them an absolute pain to try and ambush - take the ripper out first, or make sure one isn’t around before you try to finish off anyone else.
